signals.reasoning: BTC remains firm, but the edge is still too mild to justify a fresh direction under a caution regime.
ETH is still the cleanest reduced-size long because price and open interest are rising together while funding remains flat.
SOL still reads as a short-covering rally rather than a durable continuation entry, so I would keep it watch-only.
BLAST already looks like a flush rather than an early squeeze continuation, so I would not force a fresh long after the first move has started.
SPX remains an acceptable reduced-size squeeze long because funding is still clearly negative and open interest is still building, though the setup is weaker than ETH.
TON remains the clearest reduced-size peak fade because it is still very extended and funding has turned more clearly positive.
VVV still has enough trend support that I would not short it without a clearer rejection, but it is also too extended to chase.
ZEREBRO is already in a flush state and too low-liquidity to trust for a fresh directional trade.
